config.rationale: Experiment 7/30 — pivot to KV-cache compression for throughput. The current throughput leader (94f131fd3918, 7142 tok/s) already stacks chunked_prefill + prefix_caching + max_num_seqs=512 + max_num_batched_tokens=16384, but the gain over the unbatched baseline was only +0.6%, signaling we're not hitting the batching ceiling — the system is likely KV-bound or compute-bound on small-prefix synthetic benchmarks. The biggest remaining throughput lever on H100 (which has native fp8 tensor cores) is kv_cache_dtype=fp8: it halves KV memory, lets vLLM admit ~2× more concurrent sequences within the same VRAM envelope, and H100 fp8 KV read/write is essentially free. Two coordinated changes vs the throughput champ: (1) kv_cache_dtype=fp8 — the throughput bet; (2) max_num_batched_tokens 16384 → 32768 — gives chunked prefill a bigger budget so prefill chunks don't starve decode at the new higher concurrency that fp8 KV unlocks. Held constant: TP=1, max_model_len=65536 (apples-to-apples with all prior runs, no OOM risk), gpu_memory_utilization=0.92, enable_chunked_prefill, enable_prefix_caching, --language-model-only (free win, frees vision tower memory), enforce_eager=false (CUDA graphs on for throughput), fcfs, dtype=auto. Following Rule 10/11: NOT retrying SGLang NEXTN (failed 2x consecutively — healthcheck_timeout) and NOT retrying --language-model-only ablation in isolation (already measured: marginal +2.4% TTFT, -0.7% throughput). If fp8 KV clears ~7500+ tok/s, follow-ups can try fp8 weight quantization or push max_model_len to 131072. If TTFT regresses badly, we'll know the larger batched-token budget is too aggressive and can dial back to 24576.

smoke_tests.json_mode_detail: No content in response (reasoning model may not support JSON mode)

llm_commentary: The fp8 KV cache + 32k batched-token bet underperformed expectations: throughput landed at 7096 tok/s, ~0.6% below the champ (94f131fd3918 at 7142) and essentially tied with the unbatched baseline 8e036532f8f1 (7098). TTFT p95 of 51ms is mid-pack — better than the chunked-prefill champ (57ms) but worse than both no-chunked-prefill runs (44–46ms), so it doesn't win latency either. The hypothesis that fp8 KV would unlock concurrency gains didn't materialize on this benchmark, suggesting the workload isn't KV-memory-bound and the system is approaching a compute/scheduler ceiling around ~7100 tok/s. Not a leader on any of the three goals.
docker_command: docker run --name bench-vllm-3063dc62d916 --gpus all --shm-size 16g --network host -v /root/.cache/huggingface:/root/.cache/huggingface -d vllm/vllm-openai:latest --model Qwen/Qwen3.5-9B --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000 --tensor-parallel-size 1 --dtype auto --gpu-memory-utilization 0.92 --max-model-len 65536 --max-num-seqs 512 --max-num-batched-tokens 32768 --kv-cache-dtype fp8 --enable-chunked-prefill --enable-prefix-caching --language-model-only --enable-auto-tool-choice --tool-call-parser qwen3_coder --reasoning-parser qwen3
